notes: Works perfectly.  No config needed. One thing to note, when installing the game in a long line of text of the installation folder, you'll be met with a black screen when loading certain levels. That's what happened to me a few times. So it's best to install in a short text of an installation folder or the default download folder.

gpu: NVIDIA GeForce GTX 1060 with Max-Q Design
notes: No tweaks needed on Proton 4.11-3. Just works! Great performance, no issues.
title: Crash Bandicoot™ N. Sane Trilogy

notes:
All game modes work without issues with the exception of one of the large bear levels where the bridge does not break after the bear falls through it . Using Alt+Tab can cause the game to crash and close. The game engine has a bug where it will switch display settings between levels without updating the displayed settings in the system options graphics menu. This causes a massive performance hit and heavy microstuttering. To resolve this, change the resolution, press X to apply, then change the resolution back and apply again. This will gain you 20-30fps. Unfortunately this has to be done every time you enter or leave a level, warp room, or boss.

With this graphics card the game runs at 45-60FPS. Performance drops to around 45FPS in areas with reflective ice. Settings I use:

Display Mode: Fullscreen
Resolution: 1280x720
Max FPS: 60
Vsync: On
Motion Blur: Off
Anti-Aliasing: Low
Ambient Occlusion: Off
Bloom: Off
Depth of Field: Off
Fur Blur: On
title: Crash Bandicoot™ N. Sane Trilogy

title: Crash Bandicoot™ N. Sane Trilogy

notes: Must disable Fsync to fix the crashes, esync makes the game unstable. So to anyone wondering why the game crashes immediately during the opening cutscenes it's because of Fsync. set launch options to "PROTON_NO_FSYNC=1 PROTON_NO_ESYNC=1 %command%"
title: Crash Bandicoot™ N. Sane Trilogy

notes: Seems to work without problems on Proton 4.2-9, with 4.11-6 the game freezes near the end of the first level in Crash 1.
title: Crash Bandicoot™ N. Sane Trilogy

notes: Works out of the box, consistent full 60fps on max settings. No issues encountered so far, played up to beating Ripper Roo. No crashes, no stuttering, everything works flawlessly.
title: Crash Bandicoot™ N. Sane Trilogy

notes: Running proton 4.11-7 still requires disabling Esync and Fsync to correct cut-scene issues ( PROTON_NO_FSYNC=1 PROTON_NO_ESYNC=1 %command% ). Prior to Esync / Fsync being disabled experienced stuttering and audio video sync issues during opening and subsequent cut-scenes. Once launch options applied, game runs smoothly with locked 60fps at 1440p.

title: Crash Bandicoot™ N. Sane Trilogy

notes: On multimonitor setup, the game crashes on startup if primary screen isn't set or the game is launched from secondary screen. Just set primary monitor from arandr, nvidia-settings or whatever your screen configuration tool is and launch the game from that specific monitor. Crash 2 played without problems for an hour. Didn't test the other games.
